response: The internet originated in the 1960s with U.S. Department of Defense‑funded ARPANET, which sent its first message in 1969 and adopted TCP/IP in 1983 to become the modern network. Tim Berners‑Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (HTML, URLs, HTTP) and the 1993 release of the Mosaic browser made the web usable for the public, sparking the dot‑com boom of the late‑1990s and later the rise of social media, mobile internet after the 2007 iPhone, and cloud services like AWS (2006). Today, over 5 billion people are online, and the internet underpins virtually all facets of commerce, communication, and entertainment.
